title: [WIP] Add support for annotations and labels

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

As it stands, this is incomplete. You only add annotations to `podman generate kube containername` which generates just for one container (a functionality I didn't even realize existed). You'll also want to do the same in the `podWithContainers` function

Though, in general, I don't think this approach will satisfy what Kata needs. What we should really do is unconditionally set the correct annotations (regardless of source, like in: https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4368), which will allow pods created in kata and whatever other runtime to work. 

Not to say this isn't useful work, adding annotations to a container is something that exists in the k8s pod api, and thus we want to implement. But I don't want the two things to get mixed up
The original justification there was labels vs annotations - how are they different, and why do pods need both?
Agree with @haircommander that we should do annotations for the Kata runtime unconditionally if we detect it.
@mheon Don't know.  Kubernetes seems to have ignored the existence of Labels and created Annotations.  But I don't know the history.

> The goal I have is to allow us to easily transition containers/pods that have annotations for KATA to be able to move back and forth between kubernetes and libpod.

(edit, if I'm understanding this correctly, it seems as though you're saying we should spawn a pod as a kata pod in podman if it was done as such in k8s, and vice versa, based on annotations. the following paragraph works off that assumption. If that's not correct, sorry)

I am not sure this functionality exists right now in kubernetes. We would definitely want some sort of unified api structure for declaring which runtime to use for which pod/container before we go down this path.
This pull request had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the pull request will be automatically closed within 7 days.
